desc:
Short grasses grow here and continue to the north. To the west, holly bushes
take over the landscape, painting it a distinctive colour. To the north, beyond
more grassland, you can see the line of a river snaking its way east to west
with a road clinging to its northern bank.
name: Grassy Scrubland

desc:
Grasses grow in abundance here, irrigated by the river to the north. The grasses
continue to the south, while holly bushes begin to take over to the west and
south-west. Across the river, you can see what remains of an ancient road
following the river's path.
name: Grassy Scrubland

desc:
The water gently laps against the shore here. Among the reeds at the edge of the
water insects hover looking for food. The slope of the bank here makes for a
possible entrance to the water, but the waters look cold and unforgiving so
maybe this would not be such a good place for a swim.
name: On the South Bank of the Estelin

desc:
The field bends slowly down to the edge of the water here. Around the shore some
insects are fleeting about, every now and again a fish will surface and snatch
an insect from the air. It seems possible to enter the river to the east.
name: On the South Bank of the Estelin

desc:
The field here slopes gently down towards the water forming a small muddy bank.
Around the edge of the water there are some reeds with a few insects buzzing
around them. A small path leads off along the bank to the east. From here the
river looks very cold and probably is as it has come from the frozen mountains
far in the east.
name: On the South Bank of the Estelin

desc:
A small muddy path leads from here to the west, but this place is mostly filled
by big brambles. It is difficult for big animals to move here. A slow river
flows in the north and some fields seem to begin in the east.
name: The South Bank of the Estelin

desc:
The vegetation grows dense here, near the river. Some reeds are growing in the
water. The river runs north of here and an old city can be seen on top of big
carved stone wall on the other bank. Some fields can be seen in the south.
name: The South Bank of the Estelin

desc:
This field used to be cultivated long ago. Some sparse wheat can still be seen,
among the hundred of stinging weeds growing here. Also a few old walls,
separating the old small fragments, still exist, but they are mostly crumbled.
Such fields can be seen to the north and the east. The weeds are too dense to go
to the south or the west.
name: An Old Field

desc:
This field, with a dense vegetation, is surrounded by a bow of the Glanduin
which receives the waters of the Sirannon River not far in the north. The plants
here are growing well, as the sun is hot and the water is brought regularly by
the river. The river is north and east of here and others fields can be seen
west and south of here.
name: Near the Glanduin River

desc:
This field has probably gone centuries since being used for crops - only a few
hints remain to show it was once cultivated. The remnants of many small walls
crisscross the fields - probably once they represented boundary markers.
name: An Old Field

desc:
Lots of light green plants grow here. The ground should be quite fertile and, as
the Glanduin River flows just east of here, enough water should be found by the
vegetation. West of here there is a field divided by some crumbled walls.
name: Near the Glanduin River

desc:
The river flows slowly here. It is quite large, but not too much deep, as one
can see the grit on its bed. The dam to the east looks like it is somewhat
damaged and some water seems to escape by this way, creating probably a bog on
the neighbouring fields. A big river joins this one far in the north-west.
name: River Glanduin

desc:
The dark green water slowly runs to the west. Upon peering into the depths of
the stream, small fish can be seen darting around small rocks and pebbles on the
stream floor. To the north the remains of some small huts can be seen.
name: River Glanduin

desc:
This large river runs slowly to the north. The stream is not very deep but the
green color of the water makes it look deeper as it is. Some small fish are
swimming in it and lots of small rocks and pebbles can be seen on the stream
floor.
name: River Glanduin

desc:
The flow runs slowly towards the north-west where a big tributary, the Sirannon
River meets this large river. On the east bank, one can see a small village
which seems to have been attacked and burnt down recently. Far in the north
there is a big city standing on the top of a sharp cliff.
name: River Glanduin

desc:
The river here makes a small bend toward the north just before mixing its waters
with the ones of the Sirannon north of here. The river comes from the east and
to the west some vegetation grows on the bank of the Glanduin. Far in the north
two bridges can be seen, connecting the two sides of a deep gorge.
name: River Glanduin

desc:
This small landing is surrounded by rocky walls. The rocky ground, partly wet
and the slippering stones here, makes it difficult to walk around here. No sound
is heard here, except from the constant flow of a river to the east of you. You
can spot a tiny trail winding its way up from here.
name: A Small Landing

desc:
The river is shallow here; you can stand quite steady on your feet. Looking up,
you can see an old bridge, but it seems like an impossible task to climb back
up, as you find yourself in a gorge. However, to the west you can spot a little
landing. Perhaps you can climb up from there? To the south you can see this
river joining another one, making it bigger and stronger.
name: Under the Bridge

desc:
A stair was excavated in the carved cliff letting thieves reaching the Hollin
Road that runs up there. To the east there is a small wooden plank bridge which
seems not to be very solid. On the other side of the valley there is a same
stair letting the people go inside Ost-in-Edhil. The decaying mass of the old
bridge hides half of the sky.
name: West of the Small Bridge

desc:
This bridge is neither solid, neither broad. The river flows quickly a few feet
under the planks. On both side of the river, there are cliffs with stairs
excavated in the stone. The big mass of the old stone bridge fill the sky.
name: On a Plank Bridge

desc:
This is the base of a stairs leading up inside Ost-in-Edhil, the old Noldorin
city which was ruined by Sauron's armies during the Second Age. This stair, like
the plank bridge thrown across the river, was made by thieves who wanted to
enter the elven city.
name: East of a Small Bridge

desc:
This bog was probably created during the war between the Noldor and Sauron, long
time ago. The waters of the Glanduin River came here and submerged all the
vegetation. Since then, the vegetation decayed and slowly turned into peat. Some
muddy holes can be seen, indicating that some people used to come here to find
some fuel for their fires.
name: A Peat Bog

desc:
Here, the mist is grey and dense, diffusing and absorbing all sources of light.
While it is not known how long the mist has covered this land, the muddy ground
has become bare, except for a few strands of yellow vegetation that struggle in
their growth. Without much vegetation, the peaceful herbivores have become
scarce.
name: Deep in the Mist

desc:
A small creek passes through the valley from east to west. That and the small
protection from the elements given by the valley walls have pleased some farmer
who seem to have settled down here. Vegetables grow in a churned strip of earth
and further west you see more plants arranged in an orderly fashion.
name: Cultivated Valley

desc:
High grass grows on the barely undulated terrain in these desolate lands. Winds
make the whole grassy landscape undulate even more, almost giving a sensation of
sea-sickness. Beside the slight irregularity the plain is very flat and there is
nothing to hide the horison in the distance.
name: Undulating Plains

desc:
Endless green plains extend in every direction leaving you the most outstanding
break in the general flatness. All kinds of animals eat from the grass and with
them there are many sneaking predators looking for easy prey.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grass grows green here, grazing the legs of passing creatures as it slowly
sways in the wind. The landscape surrounding you is also very fertile and the
land between you and the horizon is coloured in various shades of green.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grasslands start sloping upward to the south where some hills can be seen.
The plains stretch out in the north and east, and continue without end all the
way to the horizon far away in the distance. The grass around you is scattered
with small stones and pebbles.
name: Sloping Grasslands

desc:
The grasslands stretch out in all directions away from you on this elevated
terrain, save the hill that blocks your view to the east. The colour of the
grasses change shade to a darker hue of brownish green in the south, melting
into the horizon. Half-hidden in the grass here are some white stones arranged
in a circle.
name: On a Low Mesa

desc:
This small track was used by the inhabitants of the small village which can be
seen just north of here to go to the peat bog, which is just south of here, in
order to take some peat for their fires. The village itself lies in ruins now.
The village looks like it has been burnt down and molested very recently. A
large river, the Glanduin, is flowing just west of here.
name: Track to the Bog

desc:
This hut is more or less completely burned down to the ground. The remains of a
table and some burnt pieces of a bed are all that is left in this room. A small
hearth is placed where the eastern wall used to lie. The room has a poor air to
it, and there are no signs of any wealth at all. Across the floor lie some
painted skins depicting battles fought long ago, but they are more or less
completely destroyed.
name: A Ruined Home

desc:
These Dunlendings who inhabited this village are not very wealthy as all the
furniture seems to have been very inexpensive and meagre looking. In the south
wall a fireplace stands with a few embers glowing away in the grate. The remains
of a small bed takes up most of the north wall and a small table lies in pieces
in the centre of the room. The only exit is back to the north.
name: A Demolished Hut

desc:
The presense of decomposing organic matter is strong, causing a layer of mist to
cover the surrounding areas. Here, the mist is thin and wispy, but to the south
it quickly thickens. Strange sound seem to originate from within the thickening
mist, for evil growls and wolf-like howls echo loudly from that region.
name: Edge of the Wetlands

desc:
Here, the mist has thickened, preventing most, if not all, sunlight from
reaching the ground during the brightest of days. The ground, soft and spongy,
is bare except for a few strands of yellowing vegetation. The damp air settles
upon your face, with droplets of water rolling into your eyes. The mist distorts
the nearby howls making it difficult to determine their directions.
name: Deep in the Misty Wetland

desc:
Standing at the entrance of the building, you notice foot prints in the wet mud
which covers the floor, indicating that someone has been here recently. Several
worn out pairs of boots stand adjacent to the northern wall, while a pile of
logs destined for someone's fire are stacked in the southwest corner. A small
fireplace runs along the southern wall. Even though the hut is very old, it
seems to have only begun to decayed recently due to lack of care. On the west
wall there is a small window from which you can see a small encampment
consisting of several huts and a small corral. Further to the west, beyond the
encampment you can make out a small decaying hut.
name: Inside a Small Stone Dwelling

desc:
The single room hut, cramp and decaying, is crudely furnished with a wooden
table, a small bed and a few unstable stools. The previous inhabitant obviously
had little means or care for material comfort. Next to the cold fire place there
is a large half empty cooking pot covered with mould and mildew.
name: Sparse Hut

desc:
You stand at the edge of a small encampment. The entrances to two small huts are
to the north and east while to the west you can enter a hastily constructed
corral. The only obvious exit from the camp is to the south, along the path
taken to the camp. From here the faint howl of a wolf can be heard as can the
wind rustling through the holly bushes on the moor.
name: A Small Encampment

desc:
Straw covers the ground here, keeping the ground soft and warm for the animals
that are kept here. The stalls seem too small for horses, but just right for
sturdy ponies. The low wooden kennels were certainly designed for dogs, unless,
of course, wolves have been domesticated in these parts. A rickety gate is on
the west side of the corral, held shut by a small strand of wire. Past the gate
you can see the Dunland.
name: A Small Corral

desc:
In the dim light you can see the laying boxes for the hens and the patched
wooden walls which keeps the predators away from the prized poultry. The thought
of a few fresh eggs makes you linger for a while even though mounds and mounds
of decaying feces fill the bottom of the cages and spread out onto the floor.
name: A Small Chicken Coop

desc:
The low glow coming from the embers of a dying fire does little more than
enhance the dark shadows within this shelter. The rank odour of treated animal
hides, which the walls of the shelter are made from, is quite oppressive. The
faint smell of cooked meat is also evident, but no evidence of roasting meat can
be found. Metal discs and ring shaped charms hanging from an overhead pole
suggest that an important person lives in this dwelling.
name: Inside a Rough Shelter

desc:
This small room was carefully added to the side of the main building in a
fashion to make it difficult to detect. The shelves are covered with dust,
implying that the room was seldom used. Noting the meager provisions, you
surmise that the folk who inhabit this place are not wealthy.
name: A Secret Winter Store

desc:
A rough overgrown track leads away from the dark entrance to the wooden hut just
north of here. The track bends to the southeast, leading towards a nearby area
shaded by some trees. The moorland opens up to the east but looks very bleak,
cold and unhospital from here. Just south of here there is a small hill.
name: Track Leading to a Camp

desc:
This area is quite rough and gets rougher to the south as a small hill rises
above the landscape. To the west, the wetlands are shrouded in a light mist that
grows darker farther to the west. To the east the end of a narrow path can be
seen which leads away from a small camp that lies somewhat to the northeast.
This track seems to pass through an area overgrown with short shrubs and holly
bushes.
name: Near a Small Encampment

desc:
This place, a few miles south of the River Sirannon is just west of some
primitive huts which are constructed of branches and dry mud. The huts are
surrounded by a large irregular wooden fence which makes access to them
difficult. However, just outside the fence perimeter is a small corral, which
can be easily entered through a rickety gate. To the west lies an unusual
building that is constructed of rough stone walls, and covered with dew.
name: Near an Encampment

desc:
To the southwest, you can see the beginning narrow track which runs east-west
from a dark dell in the east to the doorway of a building towards the west. More
wetlands lie to the east, and seem to continue forever, yet, to the far east you
can catch a glimpse of the far away Misty Mountains. To the south a few hills
obscure your vision.
name: Low Hills of Dunland

desc:
A moorland opens up to the north but it looks very bleak and cold from here, not
the least bit inviting to those who seek a comfortable life. However the smoke
which comes from a camp shows that primitive people choose to settle in that
land. The short bushy vegetation is present everywhere, making any potential
trail impossible to spot from a distance. A wall of dense vegetation grows
eastward, blocking any attempt to move that way. To the far east, you can catch
a glimpse of the mighty Misty Mountains, with its cruel snow-covered peaks.
name: West of a Dark Dell

desc:
The mist begins to thicken as droplets of water form on your face as you pass
through it. While sun light can still pass through the mist, it becomes
difficult to see clearly. Strange howls seem to originate from the darker mists
of the south and west.
name: Deep in the Misty Wetland

desc:
The thick mist seem to absorb and diffuse all light making it difficult to see
clearly. The shapes around you are oddly blurred by the thick veil of water in
the air. Something seems to be moving at the edge of your vision but you can't
quite make it out before it moves away from you.
name: Deep in the Misty Wetland

desc:
A thin mist begins to form as water from the nearby river can be heard as it
collides with some submerged rocks. While the mist clears to the east, where the
back of a small hut blocks movement in that direction, it gets heavier towards
the southwest as it rides an odor of decaying matter.
name: Misty Wetlands

desc:
The ground becomes damp underfoot here and a light mist can be seen covering the
lands to the south and west. Further north you can see the banks of the river
Sirannon which once flow past the brown building standing directly west. The
odor of decaying feces and rotting eggs issues from the entrance of the hut
which can be entered through a low door.
name: East of the Decaying Hut

desc:
A light mist covers the ground here, making it hard to see your feet as you
travel slowly across the barren wetlands. The mist seems to darken to the
southwest but a comforting light issues from the building to the northeast. A
trickle of water can just be made out to the north.
name: Misty Wetlands

desc:
A damp atmosphere blurs your vision as a light mist flows across the ground. You
feel chilled as the water condenses on your clothes and begins to seep through
to your skin. The mist becomes much darker towards the west but seems to clear
slightly towards the east. The chill upon your body grows as you hear a
strangled cry from deep within the mists but it is impossible to tell from which
direction the cry comes.
name: Dunland Wetland

desc:
The beginning of a shallow valley to the west, and a hill to the east, break the
monotony of the empty plains surrounding you. The grass grows a thriving green
here, kept low only by wandering animals. Mingling with the blades of grass are
small white flowers, growing in clusters.
name: Undulating Plains

desc:
There is a small creek running east to west through the centre of these very
fertile plains, surrounded by all types of vegetation. Some of the plants seem
to have been planted in rows, a sign that someone has been taking care of the
land. To the north a thick mist can be seen clinging tightly to the ground.
name: Cultivated Plains

desc:
A drapery made of roughly cured animal hides covers the only entrance to this
small hut. The walls are round and made of clay, daubed onto a wooden frame. A
small hole in the roof serves to let out the smoke which rises from a little
cooking fire, and also allows the light of sun or moon to enter the dusky room.
name: In a Small Hut

desc:
The branches of prickly holly bushes, and the trunks of short trees block
movement to the south. The moors surround you in every other direction but a
small stone building, just west of here, catches your attention. Near the middle
of the moors stands another dwelling, a little east of the stone building. The
howling of the wolves seems to come closer all the time.
name: North of a Holly-Hedge

desc:
To the southwest of here, you can see a dark area of trees and thick bushes.
However, the thick vegetation prevents direct movement to that area. Leading
away from the dark area, a faint track, that leads to a small stone building, is
visible. Off to the distant northeast, you can see some sort of stone object
next to a rough hut on the side of the river. The moors are featureless to the
south and east of here. Less forested than other areas of Eregion, this place
must have been hard-hit by many storms. The rolling hills around you seem to
extend forever.
name: Moorland by a Dark Dell

desc:
The rough moor, completely surrounding this area, look particularly damp and
uninviting towards the south. To the north, however, you can see some sort of
cairn and a rough tent adjacent and west of the cairn. Further, west of the tent
is a building constructed of stone and of poor quality. Beyond the tent and
stone building are a few small hills, and beyond the hills are some flat lands
similar to these moors. From here, the howling wolves sound very near.
name: Moorland of Hollin

desc:
The track continues through the dark dell that is surrounded by short trees and
small holly bushes. The beginning of the track is just west of here, and is the
perfect size to allow a human to pass though the dense bushes unscathed. It
seems that a nearby grove of bushes has been well tended by someone, possibly
the person who inhabits one of the dwellings to the north. There are no other
directions to enter the dell, as it seems to be enclosed by a rambling hedge of
prickly thorns.
name: Entering the Dark Dell

desc:
The tightly packed bushes and trees of this region block the sun completely,
making it difficult to see the shallow pool in the centre. The area has
obviously been often visited by someone, as human sized footprints cover the
ground, especially near the pool. The dell seems safe because the tightly packed
bright holly bushes form a barrier against the outside world.
name: The Dark Dell

desc:
The trail follows the slowly flowing waters of the river Glanduin closely, as it
dwindles through the Dunland plains. The grass is brilliantly green, and some
willow trees extend slim branches over the water. In the south-west you see a
hill rising from the grassy plains, and west there are some thick bushes hiding
whatever lies behind them.
name: Trail along the Glanduin

desc:
Hidden in a little bushy hollow there is a small campfire. Glowing coals spread
a little light, and no smoke rises toward the sky. Someone must have lit it
recently, but now the place is deserted. Some bushes between the fire and the
trail would hide it completely from that direction, but the light would likely
be spotted from the hill in the south-west.
name: Bushy Hollow by the Trail

desc:
The grass grows very high here completely covering the soft ground it rests
upon. The green carpet continues up on the hills directly south west giving them
a soft appearance. On the hill in the west you see an old tower that overlooks
the surrounding plains.
name: High Grass

desc:
Rocks and boulders are scattered on the slope making movement slow and a little
treacherous. The grass that grows here seems to wither away further up, on the
exposed hilltop, but is deeply green near the base of the hill. A little tower
crowns the hill west, overlooking the flats around. A small chain of hills runs
out southeast from here, rolling across the plains for many miles.
name: On the Slope to the Hill

desc:
You are a few steps south of an ancient wooden tower, its doors lying broken on
the ground before you. The hill and the tower atop dominate the flat grasslands
below, giving a magnificent view, but climbing the stairs could reward you with
an even better one.
name: Below a Crumbling Tower

desc:
The trail runs north and south through the great plains of Dunland. It has been
here a long time, never overgrown even though it is now little used. The stony
route it takes does not yield well to undergrowth, and little but small hardy
shrubs grow here. To the west are grass-covered plains as far as the eye can
see, while up the hillside east is an old dilapidated tower.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
The vision of the wetland fades to the west as it becomes enshrouded by a deep
mist. To the northwest you can see the shape of some sort of stone building, and
to the northeast you can see a secluded area with a dense population of trees.
From all around you, the loud cries of wolves can be heard. The low howl, which
is more of a soft growl, should advise you to proceed with caution. The hills
continue to the east, while a faint trail leads off to the south.
name: Hills of Dunland

desc:
Rough land lies all around you; bleak silence interrupted only by the distant
cries of wolves. The wetlands are crossed by narrow faint tracks leading to the
north-east and north-west where there are a few old huts. To the east there is a
dark enclosure of trees. The stillness of those trees to the east should make
you feel uneasy. To the south a broken wooden tower sits atop a low hill.
name: Hills of Dunland

desc:
The land, rich and fertile, has grown wildly and unconstrained, covering every
part of the ground. The layer of vegetation cushions your footfalls, but makes
it easy to trip over the thin stems and tree roots that litter the ground. A few
stunted trees have grown here, striving against the wind and rocky hills to
survive. To the north a narrow path leads to the east and west, straight into a
dark dell of the east. However, here, movement to the east is blocked by the
thick vegetation.
name: Thin Woods of Dunland

desc:
The inside of the hut is quite small and cramped. A strong stench comes from the
walls, which are made of large branches packed with dried mud, where some wild
animal has urinated. In the centre of the room, a fire blazes away and around
the fire some animal skins have been left to dry.
name: A Decaying Hut

desc:
Both the river and the trail avoid a huge boulder and diverge temporarily but as
far as you can see further up and down the stream the trail follows the river
closely. The land rises slowly in the west, eventually forming grass-covered,
round hills.
name: Trail along the Glanduin

desc:
The trail passes north of a clump of bushes but everywhere else green grass
covers the flat earth of Dunland. The bushes are constantly rustled by what must
be small animals or birds. Small white and yellow flowers grow in the grass
beside the trail.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
Partially hidden in the grass the trail wriggles its way through the plains. It
fades away behind tall grass not far to the east and approaches some hills in
the south. Everywhere the grass is green and undulates softly in the wind.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
Cutting its way through the plains, this trail is mainly used by Dunlending
hunters to mark the route between the best hunting grounds. Here the ground is
rather mixed - somewhat arid further to the east, and in better bloom to the
west. The trail continues southeast from here, towards the Misties, and west
towards a solitary hill.
name: Hunting Trail

desc:
The path runs along the brush edge here, in the wind-battered plains. Both the
brush and plains appear devoid of water, the arid soil little more than dust on
the ground. Far to the east the Misties stand tall, dominating the landscape
even from this great distance. The path continues west towards a lone hill in
the plains, and east along the brushland's border.
name: Path through the Drylands

desc:
This trail runs down from the long-ruined hilltop town to the west, and into the
plains. Along its southern edge it touches a large patch of brush, which you
cannot see more than a few feet into. Out north stretch the flats, grassy and
never-ending. East the path continues along the border of the brush, and turns
southeast.
name: Dunlending Trail

desc:
These ruins are spread across the top of a solitary hill, rotting wooden and
stone structures of all types. Making out what they once were is difficult, as
most are little more than a collection of stone remnants covered by moss and
reams of undergrowth. It must once have been a fairly important place, standing
as it does at the junction of four trails. The Dunlendings who once occupied it
have long since left for better pastures, like most of their kind in northern
Dunland.
name: Ruins of a Dunlending Settlement

desc:
Having seen much use in years past, this trail now looks abandoned. Deep ruts
formed by travellers carrying heavy loads now only serve to trip up the rare
careless wanderer. The path clearly leads to the west, and seems to widen
further in that direction. What lies there surely is no longer the destination
it once was, judging by the lack of traffic on this trail.
name: Path by a Hillside

desc:
Flat and packed hard by the wind and sun that beat upon it, the dirt here makes
a path that must stretch for miles. Miles upon miles of high grasses stretch in
all directions, except to the south. There a decaying wooden fence surrounds an
untended field of crops, the countless stalks wild and intermingled.
name: Old Trail along the Fields

desc:
Loosely packed dirt is all this trail consists of, little more than a farm trail
originally. Since northern Dunland was largely abandoned it is now used by the
solitary hunters as the quickest route between two points. It shows signs of
decay in places, where plants have taken hold. Not a soul has made an attempt to
clear the path, and it grows steadily more difficult to pass every year.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
The ground grows slightly arid here, reflecting the difference in height. Not so
much a hill as an elevation, the land here is exposed and wind-beaten. The soil
is a light brown in most spots, mostly a thin layer on the rock. Under the
shalter of the many boulders here small reptiles can be found scurrying around,
the only wildlife visible except the birds circling above.
name: High Point in the Plains

desc:
Patches of grass are scattered along the ground. In places, the blades of grass
grows long and rippling. In other areas, they have been cut short by the tearing
winds. The soil is dry on the top of the hills while the soil in the valleys are
moist. A trail is visible to the south.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
The plains continue on as far as the eye can see. Thick grasses gradually give
way to rocky soil. In some areas, the rocks have been used as a boundary for the
occasional campfire. A few shrubs grow here, large round bushes with lush
foliage.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
Tall grasses grow here, and in every direction around you. In some areas signs
of animal life are apparent, but mostly the height of the grass stops any sign
showing. However the sounds of wildlife are constant, rustling around in the
undergrowth without ever showing themselves.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
The plains roll on in gentle waves of grass and shrub looking to the north and
east. The grasses sway in the prairie like winds that blow along the area, miles
of plains rippling as one. The land is home to much wildlife under the grassy
surface, many burrows visible all around. Above ground the population of small
creatures teems, with rabbits and foxes visible all over.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The plains roll like waves on a pond here, the constant rise and fall of the
land all that is visible to the horizon. The grasslands are home to countless
creatures - undisturbed by man the flora and fauna have prospered. Just south of
here runs a trail across the lands, bordering an old field of crops on the other
side.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Wind-battered but relatively verdant, the land here appears to have benefitted
from the rains that flow down from the hill southeast of here. The grass is lush
and teeming with wildlife, despite the fairly rocky soil underneath. A trail is
just visible to the east, travelling north-south through the billowing grass.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
The plains surrounding the path spread out into miles and miles of grass and
small shrubs, with the occasional rock formation large enough to peek above the
earth. To the south a ruined settlement stands its ground atop a hill, the path
climbing to meet it.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
This flat land shows wear. Wind has blown the dusty dry soil over what little
plant life dares try to grow here. The few shrubs are scarred with the scratches
and bite marks of small animals trying to gleam some nourishment or perhaps to
suck a tiny bit of moisture from a green leaf.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
Scattered parennial shrubs litter the landscape here, their thin bare branches
waving in the wind like skeletal arms. Not much else is able to survive in this
poor dry soil, although a few blades of grass here and there attempt to draw
what little nutrients from the soil they can. South a path is visible, cutting
through the flatlands.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
Opening up on all sides from here are the plains, a wide open expanse from the
Misties in the east to the western horizon. It is somewhat arid here, the wind
battering any plant that grows much above the earth. A flat cropped layer of
grass covers most areas, but little else grows. To the south a trail cuts its
way through the land.
name: Windswept Flatlands

desc:
At the base of a spur from the Misties, these plains are constantly battered
from the winds that sweep down from its hilly reaches. The dusty soil here does
not provide much nourishment for plants - only the hardiest of shrub takes root
here.
name: Withered Plains

desc:
Scattered patches of grass cover the ground, soaking up what little moisture can
be found in this arid land. The dry dirt seems to be inhospitable to much else
other than these hardy grasses and the few small shrubs. Wind has kicked up the
dusty top soil, covering what does grow with a layer of dust.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
Arid and dry, the ground cracked from lack of moisture, these flats produce
little vegetation, save for the occasional shrub growing around a few sprigs of
short-cropped grass. A worn path, clearly visible from this location, lies just
to the west.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
Cutting through the plains here is a hunting path, little used now as northern
Dunland is mostly abandoned. Occasionally an old campsite can be spotted just
off the path, the remnants of a trapper's camp, but generally there are few
inhabitants around here. The path continues north into more plains and south
towards a prominent hill in the plains.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
It is apparent that the land here is healthy underneath - the earth is soft and
the low grass healthy. However the windblown plains suffer growth to no major
height, numbing and beating the vegetation constantly. Rodents and reptiles run
amok here, the rocky ground much to their liking.
name: Rocky Flats

desc:
Small rodents scurry between the rocks littering the ground here, underneath the
surface of the grass. Pebbles are found lying all around, a rocky underbed to
the soft grass. Northwards several hills can be seen rising above the land,
rolling out from the Misties in the east like a wave in the land.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Dry, cracked earth with scattered low, wilted shrubs struggle to grow around
here, even in the shade of the countless large rocks dotting the land. The
landscape gently rises to the west, wind erosion slowly eating away at in
places, leaving smooth rock-hard soil. In the distance you can make out a large
hill to the southeast, alone in the plains.
name: Dried-up Plains

desc:
The land here becomes gently sloped towards the north, where a hill is visible
rising out of the plains. Apart from that solitary landmark the immediate land
is all flat grasslands, the same dry parched growth everywhere you look. East
the presence of the Misty Mountains dominates the skyline, even at this huge
distance.
name: Dry Sloping Plains

desc:
Dry and sun-scorched earth marks these seemingly endless plains. The immediate
area is mainly flatlands, but you can see the land east of you rising to the
north. Large stones litter the area, giving shade to the few surviving animals,
mainly snakes, that try to survive here.
name: Dried-up Plains

desc:
Directly east a hill rises from the dry plains, but here and everywhere else
between you and the horizon the land is completely flat. The yellow grass and
the sparsely growing bushes barely covers the crackled earth. Here and there are
cracks wide enough to trap an incautious wanderer's foot.
name: Dry Grasslands

desc:
From the top of this hill you can see far into the dry plains in the south and
over the crests of some hills in the north. On the top of the hill there seems
to be the remains of a huge log fire. Blackened earth and partly charred bushes
suggest that it burned vividly not so long ago.
name: A Lonely Hill

desc:
Dust flies around in the air as the wind caresses the crackled earth. An
occasional bush and the sparsely growing grass doesn't stop dust from forming
unpleasant clouds. Despite the rough environment animals, mostly reptiles, leave
tracks on the ground while running from one hiding place to another.
name: Dry Grasslands

desc:
You are south of some hills, on a plain that continues all the way to the south
horizon and beyond. Some bushes are dispersed in the vicinity. Most of them are
dead from drought. Also dead, are the remains of a large stag-like animal.
name: Dry Grasslands

desc:
A lot of dry bushes grow here, sending out spiny branches. In the south you see
a straight line where the earth meets the sky which becomes jagged as you turn
your head towards the hills in the north.
name: By the Dry Bushes

desc:
The trail forms an imperfect line from south to north as it wriggles its way
through the plains leading to a hilly area in the north and through desolate
grasslands in the other direction. The grass is mostly dry here and green in
only a few sheltered spots.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
Dried grasslands start at the southern foot of this hill stretching out all the
way to the horizon somewhere far in the south. To the north you catch a glimpse
of some more hills behind the crest of this one. Directly west you can see a
narrow trail entering the hills.
name: Grassy Hill

desc:
Crackled earth is clearly visible below the dried grass and sparsely growing
bushes. Although everything is calm you can sense hidden life forms sneaking
behind your back. Only the rustling of some leaves here and a branch swaying
there speak of any other presence. Some hills rise in the north while the flat
and dry plains dominate the lands in the south.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
The vegetation is very dry here, by the foot of the westernmost outpost of the
Misty Mountains. Rocks and boulders scramble down forming a wall between you and
the rising hills in the east. Some evergreen bushes with oily leaves grow
between the boulders giving little colour to the otherwise dull atmosphere.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
The few green grass blades are overwhelmed by yellow and dried grass that in the
distance assume shades of brown. All around you there are grass covered hills,
except for a stretch of land in the south that seems to lie flat all the way to
the horizon, and beyond.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
As the hills diminish in height further east the valley opens up to the plains.
The grass grows higher in the shelter of the hills while it is drying and yellow
further south and east. Growing between grassy patches are bushes, and here and
there, a small tree reaching up for the sky.
name: A Widening Valley

desc:
Protected by hills the vegetation in the valley has grown high and dense and is
obviously flourishing. Flowering bushes, trees and green grass struggle against
each other rather than against the rough climate as everywhere else around.
Small animals and birds are in constant motion rustling leaves and squeaking at
each other.
name: In a Sheltered Valley

desc:
The trail bends along the foot of a hill in the west while in the north more
hills block your view. You can see the vast plains extend all the way to the
horizon far away in the south. There is a lot of grass on the ground, along with
a few rocks and some dry bushes.
name: Trail Entering the Hills

desc:
This hill looks no different from the other hills. In the south you can see a
small trail coming closer through the plains, rounding the east flank of this
hill and disappearing behind another hill close to the north.
name: On a Grassy Hill

desc:
From the top of a hill you can see far in the distance. Flat plains dominate the
view south of you, and in the north you see hills bulging like a tormented sea.
The hill is covered with dry grass with only a few green spots.
name: On a Grassy Hill

desc:
Low and dry bushes are widely dispersed between grassy spots along with rocks
and pebbles of all sizes. The whole land slants upward toward some hills to the
north. Despite the drought of the surroundings you can sense life around you.
The bushes rustle as small hidden animals fight for their survival.
name: Sloping Grassland

desc:
The landscape here forms soft weaves frozen in motion covered by dry grass and
sporadic bushes. In the north the weaves become higher forming hills, while they
calm down in the south forming a brown and yellowish grassy ocean. Directly
south a lonely hill rises from the flats.
name: Undulating Plains

desc:
The plains south of the hills are very dry, yellow blades of grass breaking at
each step. The dry lands seem to continue in the south except for a lonely hill
in the south-east. The spiny branches and dried bushes reach out toward your
legs as if they were trying to tear you.
name: Dunland Plains

desc:
The ground forms a large bowl here and assembled water has made the ground soft
and swampy. You can hear the squeaking of some animal somewhere in the grass.
Around you green hills form soft waves of grass, as if the ground once was made
of floating matter and then suddenly froze.
name: A Slight Depression

desc:
The hills in this area form soft waves of earth covered by grass. Continuously
tormented by the wind, the grass grows sparsely and only a few spots remain
green. Below the slope in the south you can see a green valley between the
hills.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
The hills here form soft waves of grass-covered earth. An endless wind has
browned the grass, and brings the fragrance of the earth from somewhere far
away. A green spot of grass marks the birth of a small rivulet of water that
makes its way down the hillside and down to a valley in the south.
name: On Some Windswept Hills

desc:
Here, close to the top of a rather tall hill you see can far in the distance.
Some hills covered with dried grass are in the west, and vast plains stretch out
as far as you can see beyond some more hills in the north. A small trail
wriggles its way through the hills in the east.
name: Hilly Range

desc:
Many feet have chosen to walk around the large hill to the south as the trail
takes a turn. To the north the trail continues into the flats of Dunland where
nothing but grass can be seen. The trail bends in the east entering the hills.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trails forks here, leading down to the plains in the west and into the hills
in the south. In the east the trail passes some trees and something that looks
like small buildings. A large stone has been erected in the grass near the
crossing, with some kind of message inscribed on it.
name: Crossing in the Trail

desc:
In the shelter of this valley some old civilisation has erected some kind of
buildings from grass and clay. However that must have been a long time ago, as
most of the huts have collapsed and there is no indication of any recent
activity. Around the huts some bushes have grown high adding to the abandoned
atmosphere.
name: Trail beside Some Crumbling Huts

desc:
From in between them the crumbling buildings look even more abandoned than from
the outside. The huts are in a very bad shape. Some of them are only remains on
the ground, and of those with roofs all have holes of different sizes. You see
the branches of a tree reaching out from a hole in the roof in one of the huts.
name: Inside a Long-Ruined Village

desc:
Here and there, small rocks lie embedded amidst the gravelly slope. Climbing up
the large hill to the east, the land appears to be very unstable. Venturing that
way could easily result in the unleashing of an avalanche of rocks and stones.
Only a few patches of grass, and a lone, rather stunted bush have managed to
take root here. Westward stand the ruins of what might once have been a small
village.
name: Rocky Slope up to a Hill

desc:
From this point you can see very far into the plains in the north, but a hill
blocks your view to the south. You catch a glimpse of something glittering on
the very top of the hill. You can also see a small trail that plunges into the
hills in the west and dwindles on the north-east horizon.
name: Sloping Plain

desc:
The trail approaches some hills in the south as it stretches over the plains
from east to west, but turning somewhat to the south. In the north the flat
grasslands continue, gently bulging their way to the horizon. The grass grows
high on both sides of the trail and here and there a traveller would have to
force his way through the high grass bending over the trail.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
A small trail is here making traveling easier from the east to south-west. Far
in the east the trail fades away near the horizon, while it passes between some
hills close in the south vanishing out of sight behind something that looks like
green foliage.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
The incline up to the hill in the south is not very steep but quite a few rocks
have rolled down leaving scars in the earth where spiny bushes have found a
suitable environment. The grass grows green and high, hiding all kinds of
creatures.
name: Sloping Grassland

desc:
While still on the vast plains of Dunland you can see how the trail in the south
enters some hills disappearing between two of them instead of dwindling away in
the distance as in the north. Some windswept trees, hardly more than bushes,
struggle to survive beside the path.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
The grass grows very abundant here on a slope upwards toward a small hill in the
south. A valley emerges from the hills in the west showing a few trees. The
plains continue to the south while the flats of Dunland go on and on in the
north and in the east.
name: Sloping Grassland

desc:
The valley is confined by hills in the north and south and it narrows to the
west as the hills grow larger. The valley widens to the east and the grass grows
higher. A few trees grow in the southern most part of the valley, sheltered from
the wind by the hills.
name: A Valley

desc:
The valley narrows to the west and ends at the foot of a steep slope that could
be negotiated with some effort. The hillsides confining the valley are almost
vertical, but they give way to a wider valley in the east where grass replaces
the rocks and dirt.
name: A Narrow Valley

desc:
The hillside slants heavily here leading to the east where a rocky, steep slope
down to a narrow valley exists. Rocks and boulders are scattered all over the
hillside making it difficult to manoeuvre.
name: A Steep Hillside

desc:
Here on the northernmost hill of the area you see the plains stretch out to the
north, no end of it to be seen anywhere before the horizon far in the distance.
Higher hills are to the east, and a narrow valley could be reached by climbing
down a steep, rocky to the south.
name: On a Low Hill

desc:
The grassland slants upward toward a hill to the south. The plains go on and on
in all other directions, brown and green grass blurring into gray far in the
distance. Some small pebbles and a few larger stones mingle with the grass
around you.
name: Steep Grassland

desc:
You see myriads of small holes in the ground around you. Every now and then a
small rabbit head surveys the surroundings only to notice you and return to the
safety of the underground. Passing through would require some attention not to
get stuck in a rabbit home.
name: Undermined Grasslands

desc:
From the middle of a large green ocean you see the sharp line formed by the
horizon in all directions. The land is slightly undulated and covered with green
grass around you. A wide variety of animals live off the fertile soil and only
move away far enough from you to feel safe enough to continue eating.
name: Undulating Plains

desc:
The high grass bulges as the wind caresses it playfully on its way over the
fertile plains of Dunland. The whole landscape conveys a feeling of solitude, as
if the land echoed loneliness. There are traces of life despite that, kites
soaring in the sky implies that at least some signs of life - and death - are
present.
name: Undulating Plains

desc:
The green plains of Dunland sway softly under the breeze. Feeding from the rich
plantlife there are various herds of large animals along with smaller and more
timid creatures. Ultimately these animals support a wide variety of predators
that stalk the plains searching for weakened prey.
name: Undulating Plains

desc:
The path runs north to south, through the plains of Dunland. It has been here a
long time, made mostly of trampled grass and stone. All around are more plains
covered with grass as far as the eye can see. You catch a glimpse of the Misty
Mountains far to the east, imposing even from this distance.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
A variety of plants and grasses grow on these plains. Stiff stems with yellow
flowers stand against the wind, while the grass bows deeply with the breeze. A
bit west there is a small stream followed by what seems to be a narrow trail.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grass is deeply green here, almost shading in blue in some spots. Reaching
up between the grass blades beautiful blue flowers with long narrow stems arise.
A hill climbs in the east, and another in the north, but south you see nothing
but grass covered plains.
name: Deeply Green Grass

desc:
The hill barely rises from the plain but its north side is almost vertical and
forms a precipice down to the grass below. A tree stretches long branches up in
the air in the south, reaching higher than this hill. The grass up here bulges
and bows as the wind caresses it gently.
name: On a Tiny Knoll

desc:
Large bushes grow on the hillsides and green grass on the relatively flat ground
between the hills. Stretching from east to north the great river Glanduin flows
through, cutting a path in the plains. Between you and the river you can see the
faint line of a small trail lying on the banks.
name: Plains between the Hills

desc:
The hill splits into two near the top. From here, you can see far into the
surrounding plains and you can follow the gentle flowing of the Glanduin river
far to the north where it eventually flows past the ruins of Ost-in-Edhil and to
the east where it emerges from dense woods.
name: On the Twin Hills

desc:
The grass on the plains is green and grows very high. Pebbles and interlaced
grass blades makes it hard to move, but only for larger creatures. You can see
how small animals could run about well hidden below the dense vegetation. The
whole plain slopes toward a small hill in the west.
name: Sloping Plain

desc:
The immense fields of Dunland stretch out around you with grass in various
shades of green dominating the landscape. Northwest you see the ground sloping
up toward a small and rounded hill. Small yellow flowers struggle to break
through the dense grass at your feet to get some sunlight.
name: Dunland Plains

desc:
The grass is very green and soft in this spot and it grows ankle-high. Faint
tracks suggest that many animals live here. The grassy plains continue in all
directions and far away you see the sharp border between blue sky and green
earth.
name: Green Grassland

desc:
The grass is very dense growing waist-high here. The grass blades are somehow
interwoven making it very straining to move. The wind makes the world around you
wave like an ocean, but in shades of green instead of blue. Everything is flat
except for the outlining of some hills far south and north.
name: Dunland Plains

desc:
Rank weeds and grassy turf cover the almost completely flat plain. Plants with
sprouting flowers reach toward the sky and a very large, flowering bush grows in
the middle. A large herd of grass-eating animals must have passed recently
grazing a wide track grass through the otherwise very high vegetation.
name: Dunland Plains

desc:
The grass sways in the wind, giving the sensation of being in a green and rather
agitated ocean. High grass blades with sharp edges sting the legs of unfortunate
wanderers resulting in a brief moment of pain along with a red and itching bump
on the skin.
name: Stinging Grass

desc:
A large old tree rests on the steep hillside to the north littering the
surrounding grounds with dead leaves. However there is still much foliage on the
old branches and the atmosphere under the tree is rather dark and mysterious
even during the brightest summer day.
name: Under a Large Tree

desc:
Grass grows abundant here, and along with it there are a few bushes and what
could be called a tree, but in fact is not much taller than the bushes. Every
movement results in rustling from the bushes.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
Long green grass blades reach toward the sky swaying gently in the wind. The
vegetation cover the land everywhere around you, on the flat plains of Dunland,
and on the small hills in the north. You hear the sounds of a shallow stream
somewhere to the west, but it is hidden from this position.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The Dunland Trail runs north to south through the great plains of Dunland. This
trail has been here a long time, made mostly of trampled grass and stone. To the
west are plains covered with grass as far as the eye can see, and to the east
more plains. You catch a glimpse of the mountains far to the east.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
These plains form the western border of Dunland. There is green grass as far as
the eye can see to the west and south. A cool breeze passes over the plains
adding to the relaxing atmosphere. To the east you see a small break in the
plains and to the west green grass as far as the eye can see.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The plains stretch out around you from the this lonely spot in the vast plains
of Dunland. A bit farther to the south you make out rising hills breaking the
dullness of flat plains, but in the north the plain looks no different.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The Dunland Trail runs north to south through the great plains of Dunland. This
trail has been here a long time, made mostly of trampled grass and stone. To the
west are plains covered with grass as far as the eye can see, and to the east
more plains. You catch a glimpse of the mountains far to the east.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
The plains of Dunland look particularly flat here. The grass looks as flat as a
board as each blade becomes blurred and melts into the surroundings. Some hills
to the the north break the monotony of these otherwise level plains.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
In the middle of the plain there is a depression with a shallow hollow and, in
the lowest part, a small pond with murky water. The grass appears to turn a
darker and darker shade of brown as it nears the pond and the earth immediately
around it is bared.
name: A Hollow in the Plain

desc:
The green and very fertile grasslands of Dunland slope gently upwards in all
directions. Swampy and browned grass grow in the deepest part of the depression
along with some swaying reeds, and an odour of putrefied grass and mud tickle
your nostrils.
name: Dip in the Dunland Plains

desc:
Green grass undulates softly as the wind caresses the lands bringing the fresh
scent of growing grass. In the east you can see the faint line of a trail that
goes out of sight behind a hill in the south behind which more hills tower up.
Only some kind of depression in the north breaks the monotony of grass as you
look there.
name: Dunland Plains

desc:
Far away in the south you can spot the contour of some hills, but here the land
is very flat and mostly covered with grass. Some spiny and very low bushes grow
here and there, underlining rather than breaking the monotony of grass
everywhere.
name: Dunland Plains

desc:
The flat plains stretch out in all directions for as far as you can see. Brown,
green and yellow grass melts into gray close to the horizon. Not many animals
live here on the windswept plains. In fact, not much lives here at all.
name: Dunland Plains

desc:
Animals and possibly a few boots have worn the ground away, leaving a stretch of
grass that can barely be called a trail. However the grassland around it is full
of rocks and pebbles and it's much easier to move about. The plain north of you
continues unhindered while hills are visible in the south.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
The trail takes you around a few rocks and bushes, but it mainly travels
straight from north to south on the plains. In distance the trail is only a dark
line in the grass and at your feet it is not much more. It looks like the
terrain becomes more hilly in the south.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
The grasslands slant upward toward a hill in the west. To the south you can see
more hills while the plains of Dunland go on and on forever in the north. Some
small pebbles and a few larger stones mingle with the browned grass making it
hard to move.
name: Sloping Hillsides

desc:
The trail through the wide flats of Dunland is not much more than a trampled
line of grass. To the north and east the trail continues through the plains, and
dwindles far in the distance. Southwest and far in the south you see the
contours of some billowing hills.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
The grasslands that stretch out far to the north continue up a slope to the
south. A few large boulders litter the soil, firmly lodged in the loose soil.
Between the rocks the grass grows high as everywhere else on the plain.
name: North of a Steep Incline

desc:
Here on top of a hill you can see far away in every direction. The plains seem
to stretch out to the north, a small trail interweaves into the horizon and
disappears behind a hill in the east. To the south you see a valley and beyond
that more hills. And beyond that, more hills.
name: On a Hill

desc:
Your gaze can penetrate deeply into the surrounding plains from the top of the
tower. It was built on top of a hill making it the perfect watchtower, but now
it lies abandoned. The stone walls are soft and crumbled away from the top, and
the floorboards creak under-foot at every step.
name: On Top of a Crumbling Tower

desc:
Not far from the entrance the cave narrows making it imopssible to proceed that
way. The soft earthy ground is scarred by the fresh tracks of a large, clawed
animal. The cave opening, to the south, reveals only the steep hillside which
climbs from the bottom of the narrow valley below.
name: A Shallow Cave

desc:
A very old and apparently dying tree stands here in solitude casting long,
dreary shadows on the billowing grass that covers the vast plains. This part of
the plains changes little, with nothing but rolling plains as far as the eye can
see. The brown-green grass sways gently in the breeze all around you.
name: Old Tree on the Plains

desc:
The lush green grass forms an endless carpet over these lands. Several rocks are
wedged deep into the ground partially concealed by high grass. Some of the
blades of grass have been scarred by the wind pusing them against the jagged
rocks. To the south the silhouette of a large tree stands out against the
horizon.
name: Vast Grasslands

desc:
A myriad grasses and shrubs mixed with some colourful flowering plants spring
from the ground here. In all directions the land is surrounded by healthy and
blooming plantlife. Every now and then a number of small animals scurry along
the ground, soon disappearing into the dense undergrowth.
name: Verdant Grasslands

desc:
At one time, this trail might have seen quite a lot of use, possibly leading to
another of the network of Dunlending villages. Now all that is left is an
untrodden path, quickly being eaten away at by weeds and grasses. Occasionally
the feet of a lone hunter will beat this track, but most of the footprints left
are those of migrating widlife.
name: Trail through the Grasslands

desc:
Few feet have worn down this path in recent years. The hard packed soil of ages
past is beginning to crack under the sun and rain that fall upon it, and is
overgrown heavily. The grass grows almost to a man's height in parts, limiting
visibility north and south.
name: Dunland Path

desc:
It is impossible to see farther than a few feet into the healthy grasses that
surround the southern side of this trail. The vigour of growth there is strong,
that of a well-watered land. Eastwards the trail bends around the borders of a
field of crops, one tended but now left to grow wild.
name: Overgrown Trail

desc:
The crops of the long-gone Dunlendings still grow here, thick and healthy in the
verdant Dunland plains. Over to the east is a ruined farmhouse, now filled with
the crops it was once built to tend. Round the north and west borders of the
crops runs an old Dunlending trail, now little used.
name: Long-Abandoned Fields

desc:
From the large hill to the northeast much water is gathered, and it makes its
way in tiny streamlets into this brook, where it continues through the plains
south. The water is clear and cold, bubbling over the rocky stonebed. The river
banks have been invigorated by a source of water in this windswept plains, and
on both sides the lands teem with wildlife. Above you, on the western bank, is
the remaining wall of a long-ruined farmhouse.
name: Mouth of a Gentle Brook

desc:
The trail runs north, into the ruins of a hilltop settlement, and south along
the edge of an enormous patch of brushland. The hill, solitary in the plains,
gathers much of the rainwater from around here, and west a small stream begins
as a result. East is the edge of the brush - thick, black and extremely dense.
name: Trail below the Hill

desc:
Like withered arms the thin branches of these haggard bushes have intermingled,
making movement through this area a real difficulty. The undergrowth grabs onto
you at every opportunity, and it takes a real effort to move. Underfoot lies a
dusty but hard topsoil, the brush sucking up most of the water that falls to
earth. North and west the brush ends, small trails running along the borders.
name: Thick Unyielding Brushlands

desc:
While normally little seems to grow in these arid windswept lands, the brush
here is an exception. It is a hardy growth with little leaves and thin but
strong black branches, which grow in all directions it seems. They mingle with
each other, creating something not short of a wall in some directions. To the
south the land falls away steeply into a depression, filled with more of the
same dense black brush.
name: Thick Brush

desc:
Shrubs and bushes lie scattered about these plains, soaking up every last drop
of moisture the frequent rain brings. To the west, the bushes cluster together
forming a thick mesh, perhaps hindering travel. Far off to the east, the Misty
Mountains rise up from the hills, their tops piercing the clouds. The trail
continues north and east from here.
name: Hunting Trail

desc:
Vast, desolate plains covered with tall grass billowing in the wind extend in
all four cardinal points. In certain spots some wasted and weather-beaten bushes
have gathered in clumps providing shelter to small animals and birds. To the
east, a brook can be seen winding its way across the green grasslands.
name: Vast Grasslands

desc:
Large, scattered bushes dominate this part of the otherwise grassy plains,
providing shelter for the surrounding wildlife. A small, murmuring brook lined
with ferns runs gently around the base of a hill from the east to the south.
name: Brook through the Grasslands

desc:
A large hill rises above the expanse of the plains, providing an unobstructed
view of the horizon. A strong wind blows off the face of the hill, constantly
changing direction, causing little vegetation to gain a foothold on the hill. To
the south the hill steeply descends to the plains, too difficult to climb. A
mound of stones forms a pattern on the floor of the hill, indicating this hill
has some strategic importance.
name: Windswept Hill

desc:
These are very fertile plains covered with tough, green grass. The same scenery
stretches monotonously in most directions, interrupted only by the depression to
the south. Rocks are scattered on the ground here, mostly buried under grassy
mounds.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
Tough, green grass stretches across these plains as far as the eye can see
leaving little room for other species of vegetation to thrive. In some places
various herbs and richly coloured flowers have rooted, making the plains more
coulorful than the rest of the plains. To the south a narrow fissure blocks
movement, beyond which the grasslands restart.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
A rough trail cuts through the grassy plains from north to south. Holes and
bumps made from years of rain and horses makes travelling less then ideal. The
worst parts of the trail have been temporarily repaired to make it passable, but
none the more smooth. South the trail continues over a fissure in the plains,
where the walls draw close and join for about twenty feet, creating a natural
bridge.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
A tough, green grass covers the fertile plain about you. Even though little else
grows here, one can easily find numerous herbs and flowers among the dominating,
rough green grass. To the west a well worn trail can bee seen winding its way
through the plains, while to the south a deep fissure blocks movement.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
The plains here slope steeply downwards to the south into a deep depression.
Landslides caused from heavy rains have removed most of the dirt from the
slopes, revealing the naked cliff that lies underneath. Vast, wide open
grasslands lie to the east, where the Misty Mountains can be seen far on the
horizon.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
While passable, the tall bushes certainly pose a threat for travellers. Their
thin twisty branches keep all visitors at a safe distance, except perhaps those
small enough to crawl through the bare patches. Leaves and other foliage carried
on the wind seem to catch in these large plants and stick within the branches,
never to be carried free again.
name: Dense Brush

desc:
The skin and fur of small animals lay ensnared in the thin scratchy branches of
many large shrubs. Passage through these plants is cumbersome at best. Some of
the four-foot tall bushes have formed a jagged hedge several yards long and
almost three feet wide.
name: Grasping Brush

desc:
A tiny worn trail through the flatlands borders the brush here, which runs for
many miles north and east. It looks well trodden, used often as the brush is
fairly impenetrable. Indeed to the north it grows so tangled and interwoven it
would be impossible to enter there. The trail continues south and west, along
the edge of the brush.
name: Trail along the Brush

desc:
The trail runs between the brushlands and the grasslands, the easiest route
through the plains due to the difficulty of passing the brush. Dunlending
trappers occasionally use this trail to move between their village and the best
hunting grounds, and this keeps the undergrowth from encroaching totally.
name: Small Dunlending Trail

desc:
The plant life here flourishes whether in bloom or just teeming with green. The
moist dark soil is rich with nutrients making this a haven for many different
varieties of flora. The sweet smell of nectar lingers in the air emanating from
what is left of fruit that proceed the blossoms.
name: Verdant Plains

desc:
Green shrubs and small sandy areas cover the area around the banks of the water.
Taller grasses grow along the bank leaning their way over the flowing waters.
The roots of trees along the bank give the ground support against the erosions
the waters would bring.
name: Whispering Brook

desc:
The waters that glide along the rocks here make a very low trickling sound.
Plant growth here is healthy and thick. Several trees have begun to grow along
the edges of the brook adding strength to the soil along the banks.
name: Whispering Brook

desc:
The grasslands spread in every direction on and on without a visible end. Rocks
make their place known jutting out of the ground and rising above the countless
species of plants. You can see some of the rocks have been chipped away at,
possibly in an attempt a travelling ranger made at giving directions toward a
trail or secret cache.
name: Vast Grasslands

desc:
Flowers blooming into purples, oranges, and reds shimmer here, as the wind blows
through them. It looks as though the very ground is bursting with life and
vigour. The rich well-watered soil allows a huge variety of species to grow and
live here, as witnessed by the countless animals rustling around you. Few are
visible though, hidden in the lush undergrowth.
name: Verdant Grasslands

desc:
The well watered soil here is healthy enough to grow an unbelievable amount of
varying flowers and shrub. Among the grasses flowers of purple and yellow and
orange give a depth to the grasses that flow around them in the wind.
name: Verdant Grasslands

desc:
The roots of a large old tree extend out into the water exposed in areas along
the bottom of the brook where the water has washed the overlaying soil away.
Shrubs of various size find their place around this tree. Birds often perch high
in the tree making their presence known with their incessant chirping.
name: Gently Flowing Brook

desc:
Flowing slowly through the plains, the brook is still not that deep. Downstream
it gathers more waters from around though, and becomes deeper and swifter. To
the south the plains run for many miles, flat and uninterrupted. A path runs
through them to the east, bordering a stretch of dense brush. Both sides of the
stream seem to benefit from the water, for they bloom with vigour.
name: Gently Flowing Brook

desc:
Between the black brush and a small stream, this path makes its way north to
south. It cuts a line between utterly different terrain - on one side the arid
and impenetrable bushes, and on the other the verdant and well watered river
banks. To the north it continues, making its way towards a large and solitary
hill in the plains.
name: Small Path through the Plains

desc:
The black brush here is utterly unyielding - it would take an army of strong
axe-wielders to cut their way through it. Only east, back into the centre of the
brushlands, is it even possible to move. It is wet here also, with at least a
foot of mud in the bottom, making movement even slower. Above you the sides of
the bowl-shaped depression slope steeply in, cutting off the skyline.
name: Depression in the Brush

desc:
This long valley is well below the level of the surrounding land, and it is
impossible to see anything beyond the lip. It is replete with the same grasping
brush as elsewhere, combining with the muddy earth to make movement tricky. To
the east and north the valley slopes are steep, and would require a fair effort
to ascend. South the valley opens up to the brushlands, while west it plunges
into a dark defile.
name: Bowl-Shaped Valley in the Brush

desc:
The growth is far sparser here than further into the brush, where it becomes
dense and impenetrable. Still it hinders movement, and a twisting route is
needed to travel at any pace. East and north the plains open up, a trail of the
Dunlending hunters running through them.
name: Edge of the Brushland

desc:
This place is undoubtedly the lair of some large animal, judging by the large
footprints and strewn skeletons of rabbits and deer. In its northern half many
branches, leaves and furs have been gathered together as bedding, the gigantic
size giving some idea of the girth of the lair's owner.
name: Inside a Cave

desc:
Nothing more than three crumbled walls now, this place was once the home of a
Dunlending farmer. They are a nomadic people however, often moving on to new
pastures, and the farm is long since abandoned. The crops of the surrounding
fields have entered here, growing amidst the rotten wooden furniture. West leads
out to the fields, through what was once a door. Now it is merely a lone
doorframe, standing still where the wall around it has fallen.
name: Ruined Farmhouse

desc:
This miserable hut, which is separated from the main settlement that lies west
of here, was constructed with branches driven deep into the ground. The gaps
between the lower branches have been filled with dry mud while the higher
branches have been covered with animal skins. At the centre, the hut is just
tall enough for a small man to stand, but in that region a small fire pit has
been dug. While the fire has long since died out, a clay pot full of comestible
roots, which sits in the middle of the pit, is still slighty warm to the touch.
Over a mattress of fermenting grasses lies a bear fur, probably the bed of the
person who lives here.
name: Small Hut

desc:
Halfway up the massive trunk of the tree there is a large crow nest made of
branches put together hastily. Some of the branches crack when stepped upon. In
fact most of the branches seem to be dead, but there are still quite a few thick
branches stretching out from the trunk.
name: In a Large Tree

desc:
The top of this hill has been crowned with a large and perfectly round stone,
apparently not shaped by nature. It is of a white-grey colour and probably
dragged here from somewhere else. The other rocks in the vicinity are grey and
some have been piled to protect the corpses of long dead men.
name: Burial Grounds on a Hilltop

desc:
The mist was so deep in this section of the land, that you hardly noticed this
cave before entering. The cave is large and high, the walls rough and jagged. In
the far left corner a small mat of twigs and dirt can be seen. The floor of the
cave, mostly near the entrance, is covered by gnawed bones and decomposing
corpses that have been ripped apart by strong lupine jaws.
name: Large Cave in a Hillside

desc:
The back of this dark spacious cavern is bathed in shadow. The rugged stone
walls rise nearly ten feet to support the looming ceiling, torn and cracked. The
ground is littered with bones, torn fabrics, and rusted gear from many a lost
traveller. It seems that its inhabitants do not favour visitors.
name: Backcave

desc:
Rough wooden beams still keep this passage intact, but they appear to be quite
rotten, and touching them might not be wise as they sag and bend under the
weight of the earthen roof and walls. Dust flies about you at even the slightest
movement, rising from the floor to swirl around for a while before slowly
sinking back to the ground.
name: Earthy Passage
